lessons.title: First hello
lessons.concept: Open with a simple greeting plus a small, shared-context cue instead of searching for an impressive line.
lessons.skill_objective: User can start a brief conversation with a new colleague or classmate using an observable cue from the moment.

lessons.title: Use the setting
lessons.concept: Turn a neutral detail in the environment into a low-pressure conversation opening.
lessons.skill_objective: User can spot a relevant, non-invasive conversation opener in a shared office or campus setting.

lessons.title: Ask an easy first question
lessons.concept: Use one open, answerable question that gives the other person room to choose what to share.
lessons.skill_objective: User can turn a context cue into one natural first question without interrogating the other person.

lessons.title: Answer, then return
lessons.concept: Give a brief answer with one useful detail, then return a related question so the exchange has two active participants.
lessons.skill_objective: User can respond to a casual question without either stopping at one word or taking over the conversation.

lessons.title: Show you heard them
lessons.concept: Acknowledge one specific detail before moving to the next question or topic.
lessons.skill_objective: User can make a conversation partner feel heard by responding to what they actually said rather than pivoting immediately.

lessons.title: Follow the thread
lessons.concept: Ask one follow-up that grows from the other person’s last detail instead of switching to a prepared list of questions.
lessons.skill_objective: User can form one relevant follow-up from a partner’s answer in a casual office or campus conversation.

lessons.title: Share, then make space
lessons.concept: Offer a small, relevant personal detail and leave room for the other person to respond or take the topic elsewhere.
lessons.skill_objective: User can contribute to common ground without turning a conversation into a monologue.

lessons.title: Let a pause be normal
lessons.concept: Treat a short pause as a normal moment to notice, extend, or close—not proof that the interaction has failed.
lessons.skill_objective: User can choose a low-pressure next move after a brief pause rather than rushing into unrelated talk.

lessons.title: Read the room
lessons.concept: Use observable signs of engagement or disengagement to decide whether to continue, lighten the interaction, or give the person space.
lessons.skill_objective: User can name a visible cue and choose a respectful next step without claiming to know another person’s intent.

lessons.title: Build on common ground
lessons.concept: Deepen a conversation by staying with one genuinely shared topic long enough to explore it together.
lessons.skill_objective: User can turn a discovered shared interest into one specific, mutual conversational thread.

lessons.title: End warmly
lessons.concept: End a conversation with a clear, friendly close that respects time and avoids an abrupt disappearance.
lessons.skill_objective: User can leave a casual office or campus conversation with a warm close and a context-appropriate reason to go.

lessons.title: Make continuity easy
lessons.concept: When mutual interest is present, suggest one small, low-pressure way to continue contact around the shared context.
lessons.skill_objective: User can make a specific, optional continuity move without pressuring a new acquaintance for commitment.
